name: Pico del Remedio - La Serranía Loop
name_original: Los puertos carreteros de La Serranía — Circular desde Calles
description: Tackle the difficult Road ports of La Serranía — Circular from Calles, a 40.5-mile road cycling route with 4516 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Calles are situated in the hilly interior of the Valencian Community, Spain. The region features a varied topography with mountains and river valleys, providing diverse terrain for road cyclists. Pine forests and historical landmarks, such as the Roman Aqueduct Peña Cortada, characterize the landscape. This area offers quiet roads with good pavement, suitable for challenging climbs and scenic descents. Best road cycling routes around Calles The most popular road cycling route is Climb to the port of El Remedio — Circular from Chelva, a 33.2 miles (53.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 48 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Road ports of La Serranía — Circular from Calles, a difficult 40.5 miles (65.1 km) path. This route traverses mountainous terrain with rewarding climbs and descents. Local road cyclists also love the Christian Quarter of Ollerías – Andalusí Quarter of Benacacira loop from Chelva, a 26.7 miles (43.0 km) trail leading through historical quarters, often completed in about 2 hours 25 minutes. Road cycling around Calles is defined by hilly terrain, mountain passes, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from challenging climbs to more moderate routes. The routes in Calles are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.25 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Calles's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling near Calles?
A: The region around Calles, nestled in the hilly interior of the Valencian Community, offers a diverse topography. You'll encounter challenging climbs and scenic descents through mountainous areas, quiet roads winding through pine forests, and picturesque routes along river valleys. Areas like the Sierra Calderona Natural Park are known for their rewarding ascents and smooth tarmac, while the Sierra de Utiel-Requena offers routes through vineyards and natural reserves.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Calles?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes around Calles, offering a wide range of options for cyclists. These routes vary in difficulty and length, ensuring there's something for every preference.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for different skill levels?
A: Yes, the routes around Calles cater to various abilities. While the majority are considered difficult (around 30 routes) or moderate (around 20 routes) due to the hilly terrain, there is also at least one easy route available, providing options for different fitness levels and preferences.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes around Calles offer access to stunning natural and historical landmarks. You can explore the ancient Roman Aqueduct Peña Cortada, which is carved directly into rock, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5437965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Cortada tunnels and galleries&lt;/a&gt;. Other natural highlights include the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1031861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Playeta de Chelva&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5113553" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorros de Barchel Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3028573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco Azul of Chulilla&lt;/a&gt;. You might also cross the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/829646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turia River Suspension Bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Calles?
A: Yes, there are several excellent circular road cycling routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e859994779/road-ports-of-la-serrania-circular-from-calles" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Road ports of La Serranía — Circular from Calles&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 40.5-mile (65.1 km) path that traverses mountainous terrain with rewarding climbs and descents. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33724434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benagéber Reservoir – Unbelievable road into Titaguas loop from Calles&lt;/a&gt;, covering 75.5 km.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Calles?
A: The road cycling experience in Calles is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.25 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, which includes challenging mountain passes and scenic river valleys, as well as the quiet roads with good pavement that enhance the riding experience.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Calles?
A: The Valencian Community generally enjoys a pleasant climate with many sunny days throughout the year, making it conducive for cycling in various seasons. Spring and autumn typically offer the most comfortable temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer while still providing ample daylight.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through charming villages or historical areas?
A: Absolutely. The region is dotted with traditional Spanish villages that can be integrated into your rides. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31433397" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Christian Quarter of Ollerías – Andalusí Quarter of Benacacira loop from Chelva&lt;/a&gt; leads through historical quarters, offering a blend of cycling and cultural exploration. Towns like Chelva, close to Calles, provide opportunities for rest stops and experiencing local culture.

Q: Are there any long-distance or multi-day road cycling options?
A: For those seeking longer rides, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31438361" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Remedio Pass Ascent – Puebla de San Miguel Village loop from Calles&lt;/a&gt; offer a substantial challenge, covering over 146 km with significant elevation gain. The varied terrain and network of roads in the wider Valencian interior allow for planning multi-day tours, connecting different villages and natural parks.

Q: What are some of the more challenging road cycling routes in the area?
A: For experienced cyclists, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e839892735/climb-to-the-port-of-el-remedio-circular-from-chelva" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to the port of El Remedio — Circular from Chelva&lt;/a&gt; present a significant challenge, covering 53.5 km with over 1000 meters of elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e859994779/road-ports-of-la-serrania-circular-from-calles" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Road ports of La Serranía — Circular from Calles&lt;/a&gt; is another difficult option, featuring over 1300 meters of ascent over 65 km.

Q: Is it possible to combine road cycling with exploring Valencia City?
A: While Calles offers a rural cycling experience, Valencia City is relatively close and is a highly bike-friendly city. It boasts over 200 kilometers of cycle paths, including the scenic Turia Gardens and routes along Malvarrosa Beach. You could consider a day trip to Valencia to enjoy urban cycling and explore attractions like the City of Arts and Sciences. For more information on cycling in Valencia, you can visit &lt;a href="https://www.visitvalencia.com/en/what-to-do-valencia/city-routes/valencia-by-bike" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitvalencia.com&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: A demanding but very picturesque climb from the town of Chelva to the port of El Remedio with an altitude of 999 m above sea level. The views are very comforting and the road surface is in very good condition.
Beautiful, fast and smooth road down to this little village with bars and water.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Road ports of La Serranía — Circular from Calles route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features well-paved surfaces, making it ideal for road cycling. However, given the rugged and steep topography of La Serranía, expect significant elevation changes and challenging climbs. While generally well-maintained, some segments in the broader region can include unpaved sections, though this specific route is designed for road bikes.

faqs.question: How challenging is this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is rated as a difficult road ride, requiring very good fitness. It covers 65.1 kilometers with a substantial elevation gain of 1,376 meters, making it suitable for experienced cyclists looking for a demanding challenge.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route is named for its significant mountain passes, which are key attractions for cyclists. You'll encounter notable climbs such as the El Remedio Pass Ascent and the Peñas de Dios Pass. Beyond the passes, the trail offers impressive landscapes, majestic trees, and the astonishing natural diversity of the protected La Serranía area.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this route from Calles?
faqs.answer: As the route starts and ends in Calles, you should be able to find parking within the town. While specific parking lots aren't detailed, Calles is a small town, and street parking or designated public parking areas are typically available for visitors. It's always a good idea to arrive early, especially on weekends, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route designed for racebikes, which generally means it's not suitable for bringing dogs along, especially given the distance and elevation. For their safety and yours, it's best to leave pets at home for this particular adventure.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The La Serranía region is best enjoyed during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making the significant climbs more challenging, while winters might bring colder temperatures to the higher passes. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Road ports of La Serranía — Circular from Calles route. The roads are publicly accessible, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty of the region freely.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with trails such as the Camino Viejo de Calles a Higueruelas, Senda Barcelón, and the GR-7, among others. These intersections are typically minor and part of the overall road network.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Calles, where you can find basic amenities such as cafes and possibly some local accommodation. As you venture into the La Serranía region, it's characterized by low population density, so services will be sparse along the route itself. Plan to carry sufficient water and snacks, and consider Calles for pre- or post-ride refreshments and lodging.

faqs.question: What makes cycling in La Serranía special?
faqs.answer: La Serranía is considered a 'cycling paradise' due to its low population density and minimal car traffic, offering a serene and immersive cycling experience. The region's preserved natural beauty, diverse ecosystems, and panoramic views, combined with well-maintained roads, create an exceptional environment for road cyclists seeking both a challenge and natural immersion.

faqs.question: What kind of bike is recommended for this route?
faqs.answer: Given that this is a 'racebike' route with mostly well-paved surfaces and significant climbs, a road bike is highly recommended. Ensure your bike is in good working order and consider gearing suitable for steep ascents.
